hero.name: StackPicks
hero.tagline: The curated directory of SaaS tools operators actually use
hero.description: A hand-reviewed registry of software we run at StoicSoft, pair with ServerCompass, or see our customers trust. No affiliate links, no sponsored slots.

items.name: ServerCompass
items.description: A StoicSoft product: synthetic probes, RUM, SSL, and DNS watchers with alerts your on-call will actually act on. Flat per-check pricing, no region tax.

items.name: 1DevTool
items.description: Open-source template registry plus desktop app. Clone any starter in 12 seconds, with a CLI that detects your package manager. Used across the StoicSoft studio.

items.name: Aether
items.description: Retrieval-grounded answers across your docs, calls, tickets, and metrics — with citations your team can audit. Built by StoicSoft, used internally every Monday.

items.name: Routine
items.tagline: Habit tracking without the shame spiral
items.description: Soft streaks, quiet hours, one-partner pairing. Built by an indie maker inside the StoicSoft collective, runs ad-free for under a dollar per user per year.

items.name: Halo
items.description: A StoicSoft consumer app. No badges, no leaderboards — just a well-designed nudge for the ritual you already want to keep.

items.name: Quill
items.description: A focused writing environment for long-form essays and reading logs. Originally shipped by Anya Park inside the StoicSoft studio.

items.name: Tide
items.tagline: A small, opinionated state library for React
items.description: A minimal reactive store used across four StoicSoft products. Retired in favour of the standard community tools, kept for historical context.

items.name: Vaulta
items.description: Reconciliation, payments, runway — one surface across your bank stack. A fictional template brand dogfooded by the StoicSoft finance team.

items.name: Helix
items.description: Signed on your hardware, settled on-chain, logged for your accountant. The StoicSoft experimental payments rail.

items.name: Signal FM
items.tagline: A weekly show for indie founders
items.description: Part of the StoicSoft studio output — 84 episodes on building independent software. Free RSS, $5 membership for ad-free feeds.

items.name: The Margin
items.tagline: A Sunday newsletter on indie craft
items.description: One long read every Sunday from Clara at the StoicSoft studio. 9,420 subscribers, no sponsors, one-click unsubscribe.

items.name: Field Notebook
items.description: StoicSoft’s small-batch physical product — a cloth-bound, 192-page journal from our bindery in Porto. Shipped twice a year.
